name: Mianserin
summary: Tetracyclic antidepressant highlighted in a 2026 calcium-homeostasis paper as a candidate senomorphic small molecule, with aging evidence currently limited to human cell systems and mouse healthspan/lifespan.
mechanism_summary: In the staged source, mianserin antagonized serotonin receptors HTR2B/2C, lowered cellular Ca2+, reduced S100A6/PARP1/CCF/cGAS-STING-NF-kB/SASP signaling, and improved aging phenotypes in mouse models.

open_questions: Whether mianserin can move senescence/SASP biomarkers in treated humans at tolerable doses
Whether Ca2+ lowering through HTR2B/2C antagonism improves long-lasting tissue resilience or function in older adults
How to separate a senomorphic effect from broader central nervous system or serotonin-receptor effects
